sentences.original: ALYOSHA was
the younger brother.
He was called the Pot,

sentences.text: porque una vez su madre le mandó
sentences.translationRu: потому что однажды мать послала его
sentences.original: because his mother had once sent him

sentences.text: a casa de la mujer del diácono

sentences.original: with a pot of milk to the deacon's wife,
and he had stumbled against something
and broken it.
His mother had beaten him,

sentences.text: y los niños se burlaron de él.
sentences.original: and the children had teased him.
Since then
he was nicknamed the Pot.

sentences.text: Alyosha era un chico pequeño y delgado,
sentences.original: Alyosha was a tiny, thin little fellow,

sentences.text: con las orejas grandes como alas
sentences.original: with ears like wings,
and a huge nose.

sentences.text: Los niños gritaban detrás de él:
«¡Alyosha tiene una nariz como un perro en una colina!»
sentences.translationRu: «У Алёши нос — как собака на горке!»
sentences.original: "Alyosha has a nose that looks like a dog on a hill!" the children used to call after him.

sentences.text: Alyosha iba a la escuela del pueblo,
sentences.original: Alyosha went to the village school,

sentences.text: pero no era bueno en los estudios.
sentences.original: but was not good at lessons;
besides,

sentences.text: tenía muy poco tiempo para estudiar.
sentences.translationRu: у него почти не было времени учиться.
sentences.original: there was so little time to learn.

sentences.text: Su hermano mayor estaba en la ciudad
sentences.translationRu: Его старший брат работал в городе
sentences.original: His elder brother was in town,
working for a merchant,

sentences.text: así que Alyosha tuvo que ayudar a su padre
sentences.original: so Alyosha had to help his father
from a very early age.
When he was no more than six

sentences.text: ya salía con las chicas al prado
sentences.translationRu: он уже ходил с девчонками в поле
sentences.original: he used to go out with the girls

sentences.text: a cuidar las vacas y las ovejas.
sentences.original: to watch the cows and sheep in the pasture,
and a little later

sentences.text: cuidaba los caballos de día y de noche.
sentences.translationRu: он пас лошадей днём и ночью.
sentences.original: he looked after the horses by day and by night.
And at twelve years of age
he had already begun to plough
and to drive the cart.
The skill was there
though the strength was not.
He was always cheerful.

sentences.text: Cuando los niños se reían de él,
sentences.original: Whenever the children made fun of him,
he would either laugh
or be silent.
When his father scolded him
he would stand mute
and listen attentively,
and as soon as the scolding was over
would smile
and go on with his work.
Alyosha was nineteen

sentences.text: cuando su hermano fue reclutado como soldado.
sentences.translationRu: когда его брата забрали в солдаты.
sentences.original: when his brother was taken as a soldier.

sentences.text: Entonces su padre lo llevó al comerciante
sentences.translationRu: Тогда отец отвёл его к купцу
sentences.original: So his father placed him with the merchant

sentences.text: para trabajar como mozo de patio.
sentences.original: as a yard-porter.

sentences.text: Le dieron las botas viejas de su hermano,
sentences.original: He was given his brother's old boots,

sentences.text: el abrigo viejo de su padre y una gorra.
sentences.original: his father's old coat and cap,

sentences.text: Y así le llevaron a la ciudad.
sentences.translationRu: Так его и привезли в город.
sentences.original: and was taken to town.

sentences.text: Alyosha estaba muy contento con su ropa,
sentences.translationRu: Алёша был очень доволен своей одеждой,
sentences.original: Alyosha was delighted with his clothes,

sentences.text: pero al comerciante no le impresionó su aspecto.
sentences.translationRu: но купец не был впечатлён его внешностью.
sentences.original: but the merchant was not impressed by his appearance.

sentences.text: — Pensé que me traerías a un hombre en lugar de Simeón
sentences.translationRu: — Я думал, ты приведёшь мне человека вместо Семёна,
sentences.original: "I thought you would bring me a man in Simeon's place,"

sentences.text: — dijo el comerciante, mirando a Alyosha — ,
sentences.original: he said, scanning Alyosha;

sentences.text: y me has traído a ESTO.
sentences.original: "and you've brought me THIS!
What's the good of him?"
"He can do everything;
look after horses and drive.
He's a good one to work.
He looks rather thin,
but he's tough enough.
And he's very willing."
"He looks it.
All right;

sentences.text: veremos lo que podemos hacer con él.
sentences.original: we'll see what we can do with him."

sentences.text: Así que Alyosha se quedó en casa del comerciante.
sentences.translationRu: Так Алёша и остался у купца.
sentences.original: So Alyosha remained at the merchant's.

sentences.text: La familia no era muy grande.
sentences.original: The family was not a large one.

sentences.text: Estaba formada por la mujer del comerciante,
sentences.original: It consisted of the merchant's wife:
her old mother:

sentences.text: un hijo casado que trabajaba en el negocio de su padre pero que tenía poca educación,
sentences.translationRu: женатый сын с небольшим образованием, работавший в отцовском деле,
sentences.original: a married son poorly educated who was in his father's business:

sentences.text: otro hijo que había terminado los estudios y entrado en la universidad, pero que había sido expulsado y vivía en casa,
sentences.translationRu: другой сын, который окончил школу и поступил в университет, но был отчислен и жил дома,
sentences.original: another son, a learned one who had finished school and entered the University, but having been expelled, was living at home:

sentences.text: y una hija que todavía iba a la escuela.
sentences.translationRu: и дочь, которая ещё ходила в школу.
sentences.original: and a daughter who still went to school.

sentences.text: Al principio no les cayó bien Alyosha.
sentences.original: They did not take to Alyosha at first.
He was uncouth,
badly dressed,
and had no manner,

sentences.text: Pero pronto se acostumbraron a él.
sentences.original: but they soon got used to him.
Alyosha worked
even better than his brother had done;

sentences.translationRu: Он был очень трудолюбивый и старательный.
sentences.original: he was really very willing.

sentences.text: Le mandaban a hacer todo tipo de recados,
sentences.translationRu: Его посылали с самыми разными поручениями,
sentences.original: They sent him on all sorts of errands,

sentences.text: y él lo hacía todo rápido y con gusto.
sentences.translationRu: и он всё делал быстро и охотно.
sentences.original: but he did everything quickly and readily,

sentences.text: Pasaba de una tarea a otra
sentences.translationRu: Он переходил от одного дела к другому
sentences.original: going from one task to another
without stopping.
And so here, just as at home,

sentences.text: aquí también toda la carga del trabajo recaía sobre sus hombros.
sentences.translationRu: здесь тоже весь груз работы лёг на его плечи.
sentences.original: all the work was put upon his shoulders.
The more he did,
the more he was given to do.

sentences.text: La señora, su anciana madre, el hijo, la hija, el dependiente y la cocinera,
sentences.translationRu: Барыня, её старуха мать, сын, дочь, приказчик и кухарка —
sentences.original: His mistress, her old mother, the son, the daughter, the clerk, and the cook--all ordered him about,

sentences.text: y le mandaban de un lugar a otro.
sentences.translationRu: и гоняли его с места на место.
sentences.original: and sent him from one place to another.
"Alyosha, do this!
Alyosha, do that!
What! have you forgotten, Alyosha?
Mind you don't forget, Alyosha!"

sentences.text: Esto se oía de la mañana a la noche.
sentences.translationRu: Это слышалось с утра до ночи.
sentences.original: was heard from morning till night.

sentences.text: Y Alyosha corría de un lado a otro,
sentences.original: And Alyosha ran here,
looked after this and that,
forgot nothing,
found time for everything,
and was always cheerful.

sentences.text: Las botas viejas de su hermano se gastaron pronto,
sentences.original: His brother's old boots were soon worn out,
and his master scolded him

sentences.text: por ir en harapos con los dedos asomando.
sentences.translationRu: за то, что ходит в лохмотьях с торчащими пальцами.
sentences.original: for going about in tatters with his toes sticking out.

sentences.text: Mandó comprarle otro par en el mercado.
sentences.translationRu: Он велел купить ему новую пару на рынке.
sentences.original: He ordered another pair to be bought for him in the market.

sentences.text: Alyosha estaba muy contento con sus botas nuevas,
sentences.translationRu: Алёша был очень доволен новыми сапогами,
sentences.original: Alyosha was delighted with his new boots,

sentences.text: pero se enojaba con sus pies
sentences.original: but was angry with his feet

sentences.text: cuando le dolían al final del día
sentences.translationRu: когда они болели к концу дня
sentences.original: when they ached at the end of the day
after so much running about.

sentences.text: Y además temía que su padre se enfadara
sentences.translationRu: И ещё он боялся, что отец рассердится,
sentences.original: And then he was afraid that his father would be annoyed

sentences.text: cuando viniera a la ciudad a cobrar su sueldo
sentences.translationRu: когда приедет в город за жалованьем
sentences.original: when he came to town for his wages,

sentences.text: y descubriera que el amo había descontado el coste de las botas.
sentences.translationRu: и узнает, что хозяин вычел стоимость сапог.
sentences.original: to find that his master had deducted the cost of the boots.
In the winter

sentences.text: Alyosha se levantaba antes del amanecer.
sentences.original: Alyosha used to get up before daybreak.
He would chop the wood,
sweep the yard,

sentences.text: daba de comer a las vacas y a los caballos,
sentences.original: feed the cows and horses,
light the stoves,
clean the boots,

sentences.text: y preparaba los samovares y los limpiaba después.
sentences.translationRu: ставил самовары и потом их начищал.
sentences.original: prepare the samovars and polish them afterwards;
or the clerk would get him
to bring up the goods;
or the cook would set him
to knead the bread
and clean the saucepans.

sentences.text: Luego le mandaban a la ciudad con distintos recados,
sentences.translationRu: Потом его посылали в город с разными поручениями:
sentences.original: Then he was sent to town on various errands,

sentences.text: a recoger a la hija en la escuela
sentences.original: to bring the daughter home from school,

sentences.text: o a comprar aceite de oliva para la anciana.
sentences.translationRu: или купить постного масла для старухи.
sentences.original: or to get some olive oil for the old mother.
"Why the devil have you been so long?"

sentences.text: le decía uno y luego otro.
sentences.original: first one, then another, would say to him.
Why should they go?
Alyosha can go.
"Alyosha!
Alyosha!"

sentences.text: Y Alyosha corría de aquí para allá.
sentences.original: And Alyosha ran here and there.
He breakfasted in snatches while he was working,

sentences.text: y rara vez conseguía comer a la hora.
sentences.translationRu: а пообедать вовремя почти никогда не удавалось.
sentences.original: and rarely managed to get his dinner at the proper hour.

sentences.text: La cocinera le regañaba por llegar tarde,
sentences.original: The cook used to scold him for being late,
but she was sorry for him all the same,

sentences.text: y le guardaba algo caliente para comer y cenar.
sentences.translationRu: и оставляла ему что-нибудь горячее к обеду и ужину.
sentences.original: and would keep something hot for his dinner and supper.

sentences.text: En los días de fiesta había más trabajo que nunca,
sentences.translationRu: В праздники работы было больше обычного,
sentences.original: At holiday times there was more work than ever,

sentences.text: pero a Alyosha le gustaban las fiestas
sentences.original: but Alyosha liked holidays

sentences.text: porque todo el mundo le daba una propina.
sentences.translationRu: потому что все давали ему на чай.
sentences.original: because everybody gave him a tip.
Not much certainly,

sentences.text: pero llegaba a sumar unos sesenta kopeks:
sentences.original: but it would amount up to about sixty kopeks [1s 2d]--his very own money.

sentences.text: Porque Alyosha nunca veía su sueldo.
sentences.translationRu: Ведь жалованья своего Алёша никогда не видел.
sentences.original: For Alyosha never set eyes on his wages.

sentences.text: Su padre venía y lo cobraba del comerciante,
sentences.translationRu: Отец сам приходил и получал его у купца,
sentences.original: His father used to come and take them from the merchant,

sentences.text: y solo regañaba a Alyosha por gastar las botas.
sentences.translationRu: а Алёшу только ругал за то, что тот снашивает сапоги.
sentences.original: and only scold Alyosha for wearing out his boots.
When he had saved up two roubles [4s],

sentences.text: siguiendo el consejo de la cocinera
sentences.original: by the advice of the cook

sentences.text: se compró una chaqueta de punto roja.
sentences.original: he bought himself a red knitted jacket,

sentences.text: Estaba tan feliz cuando se la puso
sentences.translationRu: Он был так счастлив, когда надел её,
sentences.original: and was so happy when he put it on,

sentences.text: que no podía cerrar la boca de la alegría.
sentences.translationRu: что не мог закрыть рот от радости.
sentences.original: that he couldn't close his mouth for joy.
Alyosha was not talkative;
when he spoke at all,
he spoke abruptly,

sentences.text: y con la cabeza girada hacia otro lado.
sentences.original: with his head turned away.
When told to do anything,

sentences.text: o le preguntaban si podía hacerlo,
sentences.translationRu: или спрашивали, может ли он это сделать,
sentences.original: or asked if he could do it,

sentences.text: decía que sí sin dudarlo un momento
sentences.original: he would say yes without the smallest hesitation,

sentences.text: y se ponía a trabajar enseguida.
sentences.translationRu: и тут же принимался за работу.
sentences.original: and set to work at once.
Alyosha did not know any prayer;

sentences.text: y había olvidado lo que su madre le había enseñado.
sentences.translationRu: и забыл то, чему учила его мать.
sentences.original: and had forgotten what his mother had taught him.
But he prayed just the same,
every morning and every evening,
prayed with his hands,

sentences.text: haciendo la señal de la cruz.
sentences.original: crossing himself.

sentences.text: Vivió así durante año y medio.
sentences.translationRu: Он прожил так около полутора лет.
sentences.original: He lived like this for about a year and a half,

sentences.text: Hacia el final del segundo año
sentences.original: and towards the end of the second year

sentences.translationRu: с ним произошло нечто весьма неожиданное.
sentences.original: a most startling thing happened to him.

sentences.text: Descubrió un día, con gran sorpresa,
sentences.translationRu: Он обнаружил однажды, к своему великому удивлению,
sentences.original: He discovered one day, to his great surprise,

sentences.text: que entre las personas existe no solo la relación de utilidad,
sentences.translationRu: что между людьми существует не только отношение пользы,
sentences.original: that, in addition to the relation of usefulness existing between people,

sentences.text: sino también otra relación, muy diferente.
sentences.translationRu: но и совсем другое, особое отношение.
sentences.original: there was also another, a peculiar relation of quite a different character.

sentences.text: No se trata de que una persona sea necesaria
sentences.translationRu: Дело не в том, что человек нужен,
sentences.original: Instead of a man being wanted

sentences.text: para limpiar botas, hacer recados o enganchar caballos.
sentences.translationRu: чтобы чистить сапоги, бегать с поручениями или запрягать лошадей.
sentences.original: to clean boots, and go on errands and harness horses, he is not wanted to be of any service at all,

sentences.text: Se trata de que un ser humano quiere servir a otro y cuidarlo,
sentences.translationRu: Дело в том, что один человек хочет служить другому и заботиться о нём —
sentences.original: but another human being wants to serve him and pet him.
Suddenly

sentences.text: Alyosha sintió que él era ese hombre.
sentences.translationRu: Алёша почувствовал, что он и есть такой человек.
sentences.original: Alyosha felt he was such a man.
He made this discovery

sentences.text: a través de la cocinera Ustinia.
sentences.original: through the cook Ustinia.
She was young,
had no parents,

sentences.text: y trabajaba tan duro como Alyosha.
sentences.translationRu: и работала так же усердно, как Алёша.
sentences.original: and worked as hard as Alyosha.

sentences.text: Por primera vez en su vida
sentences.original: He felt for the first time in his life
that he--not his services, but he himself--was necessary to another human being.

sentences.text: no sus servicios sino él mismo,
sentences.translationRu: не его услуги, а он сам —

sentences.text: era necesario para otro ser humano.
Cuando su madre le tenía lástima,
sentences.original: When his mother used to be sorry for him,

sentences.translationRu: он не обращал на это внимания.
sentences.original: he had taken no notice of her.

sentences.translationRu: Ему казалось это чем-то само собой разумеющимся,
sentences.original: It had seemed to him quite natural,
as though he

sentences.text: se sintiera lástima de sí mismo.
sentences.original: were feeling sorry for himself.
But here was Ustinia,
a perfect stranger,
and sorry for him.

sentences.text: Le guardaba un poco de gachas calientes
sentences.original: She would save him some hot porridge,

sentences.text: y se sentaba a mirarle mientras él comía,
sentences.translationRu: и садилась смотреть, как он ест,
sentences.original: and sit watching him,

sentences.text: con la barbilla apoyada en el brazo desnudo,
sentences.original: her chin propped on her bare arm,
with the sleeve rolled up, while he was eating it.
When he looked at her
she would begin to laugh,
and he would laugh too.

sentences.text: Era algo tan nuevo y extraño para él
sentences.translationRu: Это было для него так ново и странно,
sentences.original: This was such a new, strange thing to him
that it frightened Alyosha.
He feared

sentences.text: que pudiera interferir en su trabajo.
sentences.original: that it might interfere with his work.
But he was pleased, nevertheless,
and when he glanced at the trousers
that Ustinia had mended for him,
he would shake his head and smile.
He would often think of her
while at work,

sentences.text: o cuando iba a hacer recados.
sentences.original: or when running on errands.
"A fine girl, Ustinia!"
he sometimes exclaimed.

sentences.text: Ustinia le ayudaba siempre que podía,
sentences.translationRu: Устинья помогала ему всегда, когда могла,
sentences.original: Ustinia used to help him whenever she could,

sentences.text: y él también la ayudaba a ella.
sentences.original: and he helped her.

sentences.text: Ella le contó todo sobre su vida:
sentences.translationRu: Она рассказала ему всё о своей жизни:
sentences.original: She told him all about her life;

sentences.text: cómo había perdido a sus padres,
sentences.original: how she had lost her parents;

sentences.text: cómo su tía la había acogido
sentences.translationRu: как тётка взяла её к себе
sentences.original: how her aunt had taken her in

sentences.text: y le había encontrado un lugar en la ciudad,
sentences.translationRu: и нашла ей место в городе,
sentences.original: and found a place for her in the town;

sentences.text: cómo el hijo del comerciante había intentado propasarse con ella
sentences.translationRu: как сын купца пытался приставать к ней
sentences.original: how the merchant's son had tried to take liberties with her,

sentences.text: y cómo ella le había rechazado.
sentences.original: and how she had rebuffed him.
She liked to talk,

sentences.text: y a Alyosha le gustaba escucharla.
sentences.original: and Alyosha liked to listen to her.
He had heard
that peasants

sentences.text: que venían a trabajar a las ciudades
sentences.original: who came up to work in the towns

sentences.text: con frecuencia se casaban con las chicas del servicio.
sentences.original: frequently got married to servant girls.
On one occasion she asked him

sentences.text: si sus padres pensaban casarle pronto.
sentences.translationRu: думают ли его родители скоро женить его.
sentences.original: if his parents intended marrying him soon.

sentences.text: Él dijo que no lo sabía
sentences.original: He said that he did not know;

sentences.text: y que no quería casarse con ninguna chica del pueblo.
sentences.translationRu: и что не хочет жениться ни на какой деревенской девушке.
sentences.original: that he did not want to marry any of the village girls.
"Have you taken a fancy to some one, then?"
"I would marry you,
if you'd be willing."
"Get along with you, Alyosha the Pot;

sentences.text: pero si has encontrado la lengua! —
sentences.original: but you've found your tongue, haven't you?"
she exclaimed,

sentences.text: dándole un golpe en la espalda
sentences.original: slapping him on the back

sentences.text: con una toalla que tenía en la mano.
sentences.original: with a towel she held in her hand.
"Why shouldn't I?"
At Shrovetide

sentences.text: vino a la ciudad el padre de Alyosha
sentences.original: Alyosha's father came to town
for his wages.

sentences.text: La mujer del comerciante había oído
sentences.original: It had come to the ears of the merchant's wife

sentences.text: que Alyosha quería casarse con Ustinia
sentences.translationRu: что Алёша хочет жениться на Устинье,
sentences.original: that Alyosha wanted to marry Ustinia,
and she disapproved of it.

sentences.text: «¿Para qué servirá ella con un bebé?»,
sentences.original: "What will be the use of her with a baby?"
she thought,
and informed her husband.

sentences.text: El comerciante le dio al anciano
sentences.original: The merchant gave the old man
Alyosha's wages.
"How is my lad getting on?"
he asked.

sentences.text: — Ya le dije que era trabajador.
sentences.translationRu: — Я же говорил, что он старательный.
sentences.original: "I told you he was willing."
"That's all right, as far as it goes,

sentences.text: pero se le ha metido una tontería en la cabeza.
sentences.translationRu: но он вбил себе в голову какую-то чепуху.
sentences.original: but he's taken some sort of nonsense into his head.
He wants to marry our cook.

sentences.text: Yo no apruebo a los sirvientes casados.
sentences.original: Now I don't approve of married servants.

sentences.translationRu: Таких держать у себя не буду.
sentences.original: We won't have them in the house."

sentences.translationRu: — Вот так раз, кто бы мог подумать,
sentences.original: "Well, now, who would have thought

sentences.text: que el tonto se le ocurriría semejante cosa! —
sentences.original: the fool would think of such a thing?"
the old man exclaimed.
"But don't you worry.
I'll soon settle that."
He went into the kitchen,

sentences.text: y se sentó a la mesa
sentences.original: and sat down at the table
waiting for his son.
Alyosha was out on an errand,
and came back breathless.

sentences.text: — Pensaba que tenías algo de sentido,
sentences.translationRu: — Я думал, у тебя есть ум,
sentences.original: "I thought you had some sense in you;
but what's this

sentences.text: que te has metido en la cabeza? —
sentences.original: you've taken into your head?"
his father began.
Nothing."
"How, nothing?
They tell me you want to get married.

sentences.text: Te casarás cuando llegue el momento.
sentences.original: You shall get married when the time comes.
I'll find you a decent wife,

sentences.text: no una chica cualquiera de la ciudad.
sentences.original: not some town hussy."
His father talked and talked,

sentences.text: mientras Alyosha se quedaba quieto y suspiraba.
sentences.translationRu: а Алёша стоял неподвижно и вздыхал.
sentences.original: while Alyosha stood still and sighed.

sentences.text: Cuando su padre terminó del todo,
sentences.original: When his father had quite finished,
Alyosha smiled.
"All right.
I'll drop it."

sentences.text: — Eso sí que es tener sentido.
sentences.original: "Now that's what I call sense."

sentences.text: Cuando se quedó solo con Ustinia,
sentences.translationRu: Когда он остался наедине с Устиньей,
sentences.original: When he was left alone with Ustinia

sentences.text: le contó lo que había dicho su padre.
sentences.original: he told her what his father had said.

sentences.text: Ella había escuchado detrás de la puerta.
sentences.original: (She had listened at the door.)
"It's no good;
it can't come off.
Did you hear?

sentences.text: Se ha enojado y no lo quiere a ningún precio.
sentences.translationRu: Он рассердился и ни за что не соглашается.
sentences.original: He was angry--won't have it at any price."

sentences.text: Ustinia lloró dentro de su delantal.
sentences.original: Ustinia cried into her apron.
Alyosha shook his head.
"What's to be done?

sentences.text: Hay que hacer lo que mandan.
sentences.original: We must do as we're told."
"Well, are you going to give up that nonsense,
as your father told you?"
his mistress asked,

sentences.text: cuando él cerraba los postigos por la tarde.
sentences.original: as he was putting up the shutters in the evening.
"To be sure we are,"
Alyosha replied with a smile,

sentences.text: y luego se echó a llorar.
sentences.original: and then burst into tears.
From that day

sentences.text: Alyosha siguió con su trabajo como siempre
sentences.translationRu: Алёша занимался своей работой как обычно
sentences.original: Alyosha went about his work as usual,

sentences.text: y ya no habló con Ustinia de casarse.
sentences.translationRu: и больше не говорил с Устиньей о свадьбе.
sentences.original: and no longer talked to Ustinia about their getting married.
One day in Lent
the clerk told him

sentences.text: que quitara la nieve del tejado.
sentences.original: to clear the snow from the roof.
Alyosha climbed on to the roof
and swept away all the snow;

sentences.text: Mientras rastrillaba algunos trozos helados de la canaleta,
sentences.translationRu: Пока он выгребал смёрзшиеся куски из желоба,
sentences.original: and, while he was still raking out some frozen lumps from the gutter,
his foot slipped
and he fell over.
Unfortunately
he did not fall on the snow,

sentences.text: sino sobre un trozo de hierro
sentences.original: but on a piece of iron

sentences.text: que había encima de la puerta.
sentences.original: over the door.
Ustinia came running up,

sentences.text: junto con la hija del comerciante.
sentences.original: together with the merchant's daughter.
"Have you hurt yourself, Alyosha?"
"Ah! no, it's nothing."

sentences.text: Pero no pudo levantarse cuando lo intentó,
sentences.translationRu: Но когда он попытался подняться, не смог,
sentences.original: But he could not raise himself when he tried to,
and began to smile.
He was taken into the lodge.
The doctor arrived,
examined him,

sentences.text: y le preguntó dónde le dolía.
sentences.original: and asked where he felt the pain.
"I feel it all over,"
he said.
"But it doesn't matter.

sentences.text: Solo me preocupa que el amo se enfade.
sentences.original: I'm only afraid master will be annoyed.
Father ought to be told."

sentences.text: Alyosha estuvo dos días en cama,
sentences.original: Alyosha lay in bed for two days,

sentences.text: y al tercer día mandaron llamar al cura.
sentences.translationRu: а на третий послали за священником.
sentences.original: and on the third day they sent for the priest.
"Are you really going to die?"
Ustinia asked.
"Of course I am.

sentences.text: No se puede vivir para siempre.
sentences.original: You can't go on living for ever.

sentences.text: Hay que irse cuando llega el momento.
sentences.original: You must go when the time comes."
"Thank you, Ustinia.

sentences.translationRu: Ты была очень добра ко мне.
sentences.original: You've been very good to me.

sentences.text: Qué suerte que no nos dejaron casarnos.
sentences.translationRu: Хорошо, что нас не пустили жениться.
sentences.original: What a lucky thing they didn't let us marry!
Where should we have been now?
It's much better as it is."
When the priest came,

sentences.text: rezó con las manos y con el corazón.
sentences.original: he prayed with his hands and with his heart.

sentences.text: En su corazón había este pensamiento: «Si aquí es bueno cuando obedeces
sentences.translationRu: В его сердце было вот какое чувство: «Если здесь хорошо, когда слушаешься
sentences.original: "As it is good here when you obey

sentences.text: y no haces daño a nadie,
sentences.original: and do no harm to others,
so it will be there," was the thought within it.
He spoke very little;
he only said he was thirsty,

sentences.text: y parecía lleno de asombro ante algo.
sentences.translationRu: и казалось, что он чему-то удивлён.
sentences.original: and he seemed full of wonder at something.

sentences.text: Se quedó allí tumbado con asombro,
sentences.original: He lay in wonderment,
then stretched himself,
and died.
